dataset: Test SEO metadata for SERP mode analysis

pi_items.metadata_value: The Ultimate Guide to SEO that is Definitely Too Long
pi_items.verified_value: The Ultimate Guide to SEO that is Definitely Too Long For Google to Display Fully in the Search Results Page
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 24 contains the full title starting with the claimed fragment

pi_items.verified_value: Screaming Frog SERP mode documentation lists columns like 'Title 1 Pixel Width', 'Title 1 Length', 'Meta Description Pixel Width', 'Meta Description Length', not the exact names claimed
pi_items.source: Official Screaming Frog documentation (https://www.screamingfrog.co.uk/seo-spider/user-guide/tabs/) mentions 'Title 1 Pixel Width' and other metrics, but not exact column names 'Pixel Width', 'Truncated', 'Width (px)'. Verifier.py (line 31) checks for keywords ['pixel', 'width', 'truncated', 'px'] rather than exact column names.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic test data created by setup_task.sh. The input CSV file contains 10 rows of sample SEO metadata (URLs, page titles, and descriptions). The first data row contains a deliberately long page title 'The Ultimate Guide to SEO that is Definitely Too Long For Google to Display Fully in the Search Results Page' starting with the fragment claimed in metadata. The export directory is '/home/ga/Documents/SEO/exports'. Screaming Frog SERP mode exports include pixel width metrics for page titles and meta descriptions, though the actual column names differ from those claimed in the metadata.
